pysnap.terminal package¶

Terminal interface components for PySnap.

Submodules¶

pysnap.terminal.emulator module¶

Terminal emulation utilities built on top of pyte.

class pysnap.terminal.emulator.TerminalEmulator(columns=80, lines=24)[source]¶

Bases: object

Wrap a pyte.Screen and render it for the TUI.

Parameters:

  • columns (int)

  • lines (int)

feed(data)[source]¶

Feed new bytes into the terminal parser.

Parameters:

data (bytes) – Raw bytes read from the VM serial socket.

Return type:

None

resize(columns, lines)[source]¶

Resize the virtual screen.

Parameters:

  • columns (int) – New terminal width.

  • lines (int) – New terminal height.

Return type:

None

as_formatted_text()[source]¶

Render the current screen buffer as prompt_toolkit fragments.

Returns:

A list of (style, text) fragments.

Return type:

list[tuple[str, str]]

pysnap.terminal.keymap module¶

Translate prompt_toolkit key presses into byte sequences for the guest.

pysnap.terminal.keymap.key_press_to_bytes(key_press)[source]¶

Translate one key press into bytes for the VM serial console.

Parameters:

key_press (KeyPress) – Prompt-toolkit key press.

Returns:

Bytes to send or None when the key is unsupported.

Return type:

bytes | None

pysnap.terminal.protocol module¶

Helpers for terminal control-sequence handling over serial TCP.

class pysnap.terminal.protocol.TerminalQueryResponder(_buffer=<factory>)[source]¶

Bases: object

Recognize terminal queries and synthesize compatible replies.

Raw serial consoles do not provide a PTY-style SIGWINCH channel into the guest system. PySnap therefore acts like a small terminal emulator and replies to xterm-compatible size queries that guest-side tools may emit.

Parameters:

_buffer (bytearray)

collect_responses(data, *, emulator, columns, lines)[source]¶

Return terminal replies implied by one incoming byte chunk.

Parameters:

  • data (bytes) – Newly received bytes from the guest.

  • emulator (TerminalEmulator) – Emulator holding the current cursor position.

  • columns (int) – Current visible terminal width.

  • lines (int) – Current visible terminal height.

Returns:

Zero or more response payloads to send back to the guest.

Return type:

list[bytes]

pysnap.terminal.session module¶

Interactive terminal session attached to a VM serial TCP console.

class pysnap.terminal.session.SessionStatus(vm_name, vm_state='Changing', serial_port=None, message='Connecting...')[source]¶

Bases: object

Mutable session status shown in the status bar.

Parameters:

  • vm_name (str)

  • vm_state (str)

  • serial_port (int | None)

  • message (str)

vm_name: str¶
vm_state: str = 'Changing'¶
serial_port: int | None = None¶
message: str = 'Connecting...'¶
class pysnap.terminal.session.TerminalSession(service=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Manage one interactive terminal connection to a VM.

Parameters:

service (PySnapService | None)

run(vm_name)[source]¶

Start or attach to a VM and launch the terminal interface.

Parameters:

vm_name (str) – Virtual machine name.

Returns:

Exit code.

Return type:

int

pysnap.terminal.transport module¶

TCP transport helpers for VM serial console connections.

pysnap.terminal.transport.open_serial_socket(host, port, timeout=30.0, retry_delay=0.25)[source]¶

Wait for and open a blocking TCP socket to the VM serial port.

Parameters:

  • host (str) – Target host name.

  • port (int) – Target TCP port.

  • timeout (float) – Maximum time to wait.

  • retry_delay (float) – Delay between connection attempts.

Returns:

Connected socket object.

Raises:

PySnapError – If the TCP server does not become available in time.

Return type:

socket

pysnap.terminal.transport.serial_connection_probe(host, port, timeout=30.0, retry_delay=0.25)[source]¶

Open a short-lived serial TCP connection for automated checks.

The probe writes one newline to verify that the transport accepts client output and then keeps the connection open for the caller-managed context.

Parameters:

  • host (str) – Target host name.

  • port (int) – Target TCP port.

  • timeout (float) – Maximum time to wait.

  • retry_delay (float) – Delay between connection attempts.

Yields:

Connected socket object.

Return type:

Iterator[socket]

async pysnap.terminal.transport.open_serial_connection(host, port, timeout=30.0, retry_delay=0.25)[source]¶

Wait for and open a TCP connection to the VM serial port.

Parameters:

  • host (str) – Target host name.

  • port (int) – Target TCP port.

  • timeout (float) – Maximum time to wait.

  • retry_delay (float) – Delay between connection attempts.

Returns:

Reader and writer stream objects.

Raises:

PySnapError – If the TCP server does not become available in time.

Return type:

tuple[StreamReader, StreamWriter]
